1639845856-12.name: Harper's Bend
1639845856-12.notes: Popular site for fishing salmon and steelhead.

1639846035-90.name: Paterson Boat Ramp
1639846035-90.notes: Gently sloping rock into water, no real ramp.

1639845951-52.name: Hood River Marina
1639845951-52.notes: Closes at dusk, no overnight camping or parking. A group shelter can be reserved for a fee. Marina slips are for monthly and annual tenants.The guest dock has a 3 day maximum, and a fee.

1639845931-43.name: Home Valley
1639845931-43.notes: Baseball fields, playground equipment, windsurfing, horseshoe pits, beach, wetlands to observe.

1639845911-34.name: Bonneville Portage Downstream/Hamilton Island Boat Ramp

1639846046-95.name: Nugent Park
1639846046-95.notes: Nugent Park is on the Umatilla River, one mile up from the confluence with the Columbia River.

1639846139-137.name: Windust
1639846139-137.notes: No wake area around swimming area. Pets must be kept on a leash.

1639846011-79.name: Le Page Park
1639846011-79.notes: Reservations need to be made 4 days in advance. On weekdays, minimum stay length is 2 days, and on weekends and holidays you must stay for at least 3 days. No off road vehicles.

1639846154-144.name: Lyons Ferry Marina
1639846154-144.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited.

1639846028-87.name: Crow Butte Park
1639846028-87.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash

1639846090-115.name: Wye Park
1639846090-115.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited.

1639846088-114.name: Columbia Park
1639846088-114.notes: Campgrounds are closed until further notice (May 16, 2008)

1639846059-101.name: McNary Beach Park
1639846059-101.notes: Take a stroll on the Lewis & Clark Commemorative Trail

1639846017-82.name: Roosevelt Park
1639846017-82.notes: Popular spot for windsurfing, kiteboarding, and swimming.

1639846163-148.name: Riparia
1639846163-148.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited. No open fires allowed from June 10 through October 10.

1639845924-40.name: Cascade Boat Launch & Pebble Beach
1639845924-40.notes: The Cascade Boat Launch and Pebble Beach are separate sites. However, they are within walking distance on each other. Dog must be on a leash.

1639846202-166.name: Wawawai Landing
1639846202-166.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited. No open fires allowed from June 10 through October 10. Charcoal and propane fires are acceptable.

1639846218-173.name: Greenbelt Ramp
1639846218-173.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited. No open fires allowed. Charcoal and propane fires are acceptable. Public telephone available.

1639846121-129.name: Charbonneau Park
1639846121-129.campingFee: 18$ for a regular site, and $8 for overflow sites.
1639846121-129.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Hunting is prohibited inside the developed park. Fire pits, grills, and hot showers available, along with a trailer dump station.

1639846209-169.name: Chief Timothy Park
1639846209-169.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited. Golden Age and Access Passports will not be accepted. No open fires allowed from June 10 through October 10.

1639846207-168.name: Nisqually John Landing
1639846207-168.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited. No open fires allowed from June 10 through October 10. Charcoal and propane fires are acceptable.

1639846125-131.name: Big Flat
1639846125-131.notes: Hunting is allowed for waterfowl, upland game birds and deer with shotgun only. Hunting seasons and hours are set by WDFW.

1639846070-106.name: Walla Walla Yacht Club
1639846070-106.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. The marina is private and open only to club members. A sani-can is available at the boat ramp.

1639846136-136.name: Walker
1639846136-136.notes: Camping space is somewhat limited and there are no designated spots.

1639846103-121.name: Sacajawea State Park
1639846103-121.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Restrooms closed Oct-Mar. Campsite accomodates 8 on first come first serve basis. Parties larger than 8 call: (506) 545-2361

1639845920-38.name: Port of Cascade Locks Marine Park
1639845920-38.notes: No campfires in the campground. Enjoy a playground, museum, and visitor center. Sternwheelers depart from this park.

1639846275-196.name: East Clearwater Park
1639846275-196.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited.

1639846156-145.name: Lyons Ferry Park
1639846156-145.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited. A snack bar is available May 25th through Labor Day.

1639846079-110.name: Two Rivers Park
1639846079-110.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Hunting is prohibited. Public phones are available.

1639846161-147.name: Texas Rapids
1639846161-147.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited. No open fires allowed from June 10 through October 10. Charcoal and propane fires are acceptable.

1639845960-56.name: Swell City
1639845960-56.notes: Windsurfing and kiteboarding area. Day use fee of $4 per person.

1639845993-71.name: Peach Beach
1639845993-71.notes: Landing only for hand-carried boats, other boats use Maryhill State Park boat ramp. Privately owned RV Park.

1639845946-50.name: Koberg Beach
1639845946-50.notes: Adjacent tribal in lieu fishing site with boat launch and picnic area not open to the public.

1639845962-57.name: Rock Creek
1639845962-57.notes: Very primitive area, windsurfing, entrance fee, gravel entrance road and parking lot, blind turn into parking lot under bridge. Only one lane available.

1639846172-152.name: Little Goose Landing
1639846172-152.notes: Hunting is prohibited. No open fires allowed from June 10 to October 10. Charcoal and propane fires allowed year round.

1639846189-160.name: Boyer Park and Marina
1639846189-160.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Aircraft landing field located adjacent to park.

1639845937-46.name: Spring Creek Fish Hatchery Recreation Area
1639845937-46.notes: Windsurfing and kiteboarding spot. No overnight camping nor parking. Day use fee. Visitor center at the fish hatchery. Beach launch only, kayaks possible.

1639846042-93.name: Irrigon Marina Park
1639846042-93.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash.

1639846141-138.name: Matthews
1639846141-138.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Hunting is prohibited.

1639846213-171.name: Hells Canyon Marina
1639846213-171.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited. No open fires allowed. Charcoal and propane fires are acceptable.

1639845985-67.name: Avery Park
1639845985-67.notes: Avery Park is a shared use

1639846198-164.name: Offield Landing
1639846198-164.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited. No open fires allowed from June 10 through October 10. Charcoal and propane fires are acceptable Boat launch is free of charge.

1639845854-11.name: Madame Dorian
1639845854-11.notes: Madame Dorian sits at the confluence of the Walla Walla River and the Columbia. Paddlers and shallow draft boats will enjoy exploring the wetlands of the Walla Walla delta.

1639846180-156.name: Willow Landing
1639846180-156.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Hunting is prohibited.

1639845966-59.name: Mayer State Park
1639845966-59.notes: Migratory bird hunting when in season. Day use fee of $3.

1639846152-143.name: Ayer Boat Basin
1639846152-143.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Hunting is prohibited. Undesignated camping sites can accommodate approximately 20 tents or RV's.

1639846033-89.name: Boardman Marine Park
1639846033-89.notes: Pets must be on a leash.

1639846085-113.name: Clover Island Boat Ramp
1639846085-113.notes: Adjacent to the Clover Island Inn.

1639846044-94.name: Plymouth Park
1639846044-94.notes: Fire pits and grills and hot showers.

1639846205-167.name: Blyton Landing
1639846205-167.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited. No open fires allowed from June 10 through October 10. Charcoal and propane fires are acceptable.

1639846187-159.name: Illia Landing
1639846187-159.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Hunting is prohibited.

1639846061-102.name: Hat Rock State Park
1639846061-102.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Private campground, store, and café nearby, for information visit www.hatrockcampground.com

1639846150-142.name: Devils Bench
1639846150-142.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited.

1639846092-116.name: Chiawana Park
1639846092-116.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash.

1639846178-155.name: Central Ferry Park
1639846178-155.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Golden Age and Access Passports will not be accepted. Hot shower available.

1639846266-192.name: Area adjacent to railroad bridge just upstream from Spalding on old highway

1639845964-58.name: Rock Creek Natural Preserve and Beach
1639845964-58.notes: Park only in the gravel area between I-84 and the Railroad Tracks. Overflow parking is East of the Mosier sewage treatment plant parallel to the Railroad Tracks. Parking fees are $5 a day.

1639846200-165.name: Wawawai County Park
1639846200-165.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited. No open fires allowed from June 10 through October 10. Water available April 15 to October 15.

1639845898-29.name: Lambi Creek
1639845898-29.notes: This is the last launching/landing spot for hand-carried boats before Central Ferry, nearly twenty miles downstream.

1639845922-39.name: Marina Park( Cascade Locks Boat Ramp)

1639845935-45.name: Drano Lake
1639845935-45.notes: Boat launch and parking lot only. Day use fee. No overnight camping nor parking.

1639845926-41.name: Bob's Beach
1639845926-41.notes: Changing rooms available. Adjacent to Columbia Gorge Riverside Lodge.

1639846081-111.name: Pasco Boat Basin
1639846081-111.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash.

1639845989-69.name: Heritage Landing State Park
1639845989-69.notes: Caution: access from Columbia River means boating upstream on the Deshutes River. Deschutes Boater Pass may be required, call 800-551-6949. Camping across river at Deschutes State Recreation Area.

1639846128-132.name: Fishhook Park
1639846128-132.notes: No wake area around swimming area. Reservations are accepted for group shelter by calling 509-547-2048.

1639846288-202.name: LenoreAccess
1639846288-202.notes: Today the site is covered by highly maintained lawns and trees, but was once a Nez Perce village. An archeological excavation revealed continuous habitation at this site for over 8,000 years.

1639846020-83.name: Port of Arlington Marina
1639846020-83.notes: Payment is $15/night for RVs and $7/night otherwise. Only 2 tent sites available.

1639846048-96.name: Umatilla Marina Park
1639846048-96.notes: Pets must be kept on a leash. Horses and hunting are prohibited.

1639846123-130.name: Levey Park
1639846123-130.notes: Reservations are accepted for group shelter by calling 509-547-2048 during weekday business hours, but not more than 3 months in advance. A $55.00 reservation fee is required.

1639846108-123.name: Hood Park
1639846108-123.notes: Pets must be on leash, and horses/hunting are prohibited. Reservations accepted for group shelters by calling (509)-547-2048, but cannot be made more than 3 months in advance.

1639846299-207.name: Pink House
1639846299-207.notes: Maximum of a 14 day stay. Handicap accessible.
